I had Text cards in place, The AI tool can't create Text Cards or Organize the Dashboard. around those cards. Community question: GDPR compliant consent not being remembered in NextJS
Reply here: https://posthog.com/questions/gdpr-compliant-consent-not-being-remembered-in-next-js
* * *

I am implementing a GDPR-compliant consent flow in a Next.js (App Router) project using posthog-js, but I’m running into a persistence issue. Even after calling `opt_in_capturing()` or `opt_out_capturing()`, the choice is lost upon page refresh, causing the consent banner to reappear every time.

Here is the init:

    posthog.init(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY!, {
      api_host: '/ph',
      ui_host: 'https://eu.posthog.com',
      defaults: '2025-11-30',
      opt_out_capturing_by_default: true,
      cookieless_mode: 'on_reject',
      autocapture: false,
      capture_pageview: true,
      opt_out_capturing_persistence_type: 'localStorage',
      persistence: 'localStorage',
    })

The Issue:

- User arrives → `get_explicit_consent_status()` is pending (Banner shows).
- User clicks "Accept" or "Decline" → I call `opt_in_capturing()` or `opt_out_capturing()`.
- I can see the state change in LocalStorage.
- On Page Refresh, the LocalStorage KV has disappeared, and the banner shows again.

Is there something wrong with my configuration or something else I am missing? Thanks

Feature request: Configurable bin width for funnel Time to Convert histogram
### Feature request

Title
Feature request: Configurable bin width for funnel Time to Convert histogram
## Is your feature request related to a problem?

The Time to Convert histogram calculates bin width dynamically based on max conversion time ÷ number of bins. This means bin boundaries shift when the data distribution changes, making it harder to track trends over time or compare across date ranges.
## Describe the solution you'd like

Option to set a fixed bin width (e.g., "1 minute per bin") instead of only choosing the number of bins. This would keep bin boundaries stable for consistent trend analysis.
## Describe alternatives you've considered
SQL insights
## Additional context
From https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/00000

```shell

```

Feature request: Median and percentile options for funnel Time to Convert
### Feature request

Title:
Feature request: Median and percentile options for funnel Time to Convert
## Is your feature request related to a problem?
The Time to Convert view only shows average conversion time as a summary stat. Averages can be skewed by outliers, making median or percentiles (P50, P90, P99) more useful for understanding typical user behavior.

## Describe the solution you'd like
Add aggregation options (median, P90, P99, etc.) alongside or instead of average in the Time to Convert summary.
## Describe alternatives you've considered
SQL insights

One way to do it could be adding an image beacon with the available HTML block, when the user opens the email and loads the image, it queries an API that sends an event to PostHog for that workflow step.

Ability for "reverse" funnels
### Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem?
No

## Describe the solution you'd like
When attempting to track why a user may have stopped using an app, it would be helpful to be able to set an end point for a Funnel report that works backwards through the final events of users that hit the selected endpoint (Application Backgrounded, for example). You can then create an inactive user cohort, allowing you to get an idea of what might have caused those users to go inactive (hitting a paywall, for example).

## Describe alternatives you've considered
Custom SQL could be used for this that generates a report, but it's not as easy to visualize common funnel paths.

## Additional context
Asked if this was possible via support ticket #44441, and was told it isn't at this time and was directed to report here :)

Feature Request: Better client/server integration for Next.js [bootstrapped] flags
### Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem?

Our current advice is to call `init` inside the Next.js `instrumentation-client.ts`. The problem with this is that `instrumentation-client.ts` only runs once per app launch - i.e. on initial page load. Therefore for subsequent navigations, feature flags won't get refreshed.

We also recommend using Next.js middleware for bootstrapping feature flags, usually by passing the bootstrap flag data on a cookie. Middleware runs on every request, but because flags have already been bootstrapped there's no way to use middleware to update the flags and take advantage of e.g. local evaluation.

There's also a risk that for users with long running sessions (i.e. because `instrumentation-client.ts` is only called once at startup), flags could get stale and there's no way to force a refresh.

## Describe the solution you'd like

* PostHog provided middleware so that users don't need to implement this for themselves
* Automatically update flags stored by the client when the middleware returns updated values
* PostHog's React hooks should automatically account for this so that the flag updates from the server are completely abstracted from users

## Describe alternatives you've considered

You can force a refresh of flags client side, but users have to think about this up front and it might not be an obvious consideration.

## Additional context
From: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/00000

PostHog AI generates a JavaScript error when asked a question while the user is actively using the SQL Editor. The issue occurs when a user has executed a query in the SQL Editor and subsequently asks the PostHog AI (via the right panel) a question that results in a secondary SQL query being generated and executed. The error appears to be a client-side crash related to model creation or instance initialization (referencing `createModel` and `createInstance` in the application chunks). 

Steps to reproduce:
1. Open the SQL editor, enter and execute a query (e.g., `SELECT * FROM events LIMIT 1`).
2. Open the PostHog AI sidebar.
3. Ask the AI a question that triggers a SQL query (e.g., "what's the revenue for the past 52 weeks?").
4. An application error occurs during the execution of the AI-generated query.

Feature request for the PostHog .NET SDK to support an external or shared cache provider (such as Redis) for local evaluation of feature flags. Currently, the .NET SDK only supports an in-memory local cache, which leads to inconsistent feature flag states across distributed environments where multiple application instances are running. When a client’s requests are handled by different instances, the lack of a shared state can cause "flickering" or inconsistent rollouts. The request seeks an officially supported way to configure a distributed cache to ensure strict consistency of feature flag states per client across all instances in a .NET deployment.

Posthog-Native Push notification support
Workflow customers make use of email heavily today, but a Native PostHog push notification channel is a huge value add.

Ideally PostHog will handle
- Device token collection (via PostHog SDKs) on Android, iOS, Flutter, React Native and (depending on user demand) Web
- Mapping device tokens to PostHog persons for convenience in Workflow configuration
- Feedback of push notification delivery and/or engagement (again requires PostHog SDK use)

Here is the implementation plan for the first iteration of Push in Workflows: https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/issues/45009

1. Explain what each series is

3. Option to disable specific series in the chart. In some instances it is possible that one series could be much greater than others, making it difficult to read the chart for the other metrics. Being able to disable certain series metrics could alleviate that issue when a user encounters it.

PostHog AI (Max) incorrectly reports successful creation of insights even when the process fails due to API validation errors. The issue occurs because the AI generates insight payloads that lack the mandatory "query" wrapper, sending the query object (e.g., TrendsQuery) at the root level instead. This results in a "PydanticOutputParserException" with the error message "Field required" at location ["query"]. 

As a result, the AI provides users with names and links to insights that were never actually created in the database. The problem is observed when users ask the AI to generate trends or other insights. The core fix requires ensuring the AI assistant wraps the insight definition within a "query" field to satisfy API validation requirements.

This feature request seeks to add a "2 weeks" (bi-weekly) interval option to Lifecycle insights, which currently only supports hour, day, week, and month intervals. The user tracks venue lifecycle metrics (new, returning, resurrecting, and dormant states) and requires a 14-day cadence to match their business model and venue engagement expectations. Existing weekly intervals are too granular and noisy, while monthly intervals hide important activity changes. Adding this option would allow the user to better align analytics with their customer success workflows and accurately monitor venue health without resorting to custom SQL queries that lack the native lifecycle visualization.

chore(flags): Investigate Flags HyperCache overlap with Flag Definitions HyperCache
### Bug Description

The `/flags` service reads feature flags for a team from a flags Hypercache (Redis -> S3 fallback). We also have a separate Flag Definitions Hypercache used to populate responses for `/local_evaluation`.

The question here is why are there two? Is it possible for one to build off the other? For example, could the flags hypercache be replaced by the flag definitions with cohorts cache?

The goal of this issue is to investigate this question and either come up with an implementation if the conclusion is we should consolidate. If we shouldn't consolidate, then we should close this issue.

Feature Request: Improve date picker UX with year/month navigation and manual date entry
### Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem?

The current date picker requires excessive clicks when selecting dates far in the past or future. For example, selecting a date 12 months ago requires clicking the back arrow 12+ times. This creates unnecessary friction for common use cases like year-over-year comparisons or historical data analysis.

## Describe the solution you'd like
Enhance the date picker with:

- Hierarchical navigation - Allow users to zoom out to month/year views and drill back down, similar to standard date pickers (e.g., clicking on the month/year header to select different years, then months, then days)

- Manual date entry - Add a text input field where users can type dates directly in a standard format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD or MM/DD/YYYY)

- Quick jump controls - Consider adding year forward/back buttons alongside the existing month controls


## Describe alternatives you've considered
Using the existing date range presets (Last 30 days, Last 90 days, etc.) - but these don't cover all use cases


## Additional context
From: https://posthoghelp.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/00000

Dashboard card "Show details" displays wrong event name when using "All events" with SQL aggregation ### Bug Description When creating a trends insight that uses "All events" with a custom SQL aggregation (e.g., uniq(properties.something)), the dashboard card's "Show details" modal incorrectly displays a specific event name (like "Pageview") instead of showing "All events" as selected. ## How to reproduce 1. Create a new trends insight in PostHog 2. Select "All events" as the event source (not a specific event like Pageview) 3. Configure a custom SQL aggregation such as uniq(properties.something) 4. Save the insight and add it to a dashboard 5. View the dashboard and click "Show details" on the insight card 6. Observe that it displays a specific event name (e.g., "Pageview") instead of "All events" ## Additional context - Occurs on PostHog Cloud - Currently viewing dashboard ID: 000000 (Product Health) - The issue is specifically with the "Show details" modal display on dashboard cards, not with the insight editor itself ### Debug info ```shell - [x] PostHog Cloud, Debug information: Session: https://us.posthog.com/project/sTMFPsFhdP1Ssg/replay/019bb78d-fd97-7625-9147-a3b03c5b2d69?t=3810 Admin: http://go/adminOrgEU/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003 (project ID 000000) - [ ] PostHog Hobby self-hosted with `docker compose`, version/commit: [please provide] - [ ] PostHog self-hosted with Kubernetes (deprecated, see [`Sunsetting Kubernetes support`](https://posthog.com/blog/sunsetting-helm-support-posthog)), version/commit: [please provide] ```

Nuxt 4 SSR applications using the bootstrap pattern experience feature flag variant inconsistency during hydration, leading to "multiple exposures" warnings and UI flickering. Although the application correctly fetches flags on the server via `posthog-node` and passes them to the client-side `posthog-js` via a shared state, the client-side `onFeatureFlags` listener frequently triggers after hydration with different variant values than those provided in the bootstrap configuration. This occurs despite using a stable `distinctId` (derived from Supabase or a persistent cookie) and setting `advanced_disable_feature_flags_on_first_load: true`. The issue results in polluted experiment data and suggests a discrepancy between how variants are evaluated or cached between the Node.js and JS SDKs during the SSR handoff. Affected versions include `posthog-js` ^1.310.1 and `posthog-node` ^5.18.0.

feat(flags): prevent failed flag evaluations from overwriting cached values
Currently, we allow cached flag overwrites on SDKs during transient errors on flag evaluations. Here is a breakdown of how this might happen:

1. `/flags` endpoint fails to evaluate a specific flag due to some transient error, like a DB timeout
2. The flag that failed to be evaluated is returned in the payload, from the `FlagDetails::create_error` function, as well as a top-level `errorsWhileComputingFlags` signal (coarse-grained, only indicates that something went wrong). This returns the failed flags as `enabled: false` [[source](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/master/rust/feature-flags/src/api/types.rs#L421-L438)].
3. If the `errorsWhileComputingFlags` signal is true, the SDK will overwrite the flags by upserting them (it does something like `{ ...cached, ...new }`) [[source](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog-js/blob/main/packages/core/src/posthog-core.ts#L649-L652)]. That wouldn't be an issue if we omitted the flags with issues, but we don't (and I don't think we should, as the original issue proposes)
4. After the overwrite, a flag that was set to true will be changed to false.

We can split this issue into two steps.

- [x] Add more detail to the flags that failed to be evaluated using the `reason.description` field, that is currently just set to `None`, and return a more fine-grained `failed: boolean` field each one of the flags.
* In the SDKs side, use the failed field to filter out just the flags that failed to be evaluated before applying the merge.
    - [x] JS-SDK
    - [x] Go-SDK
    - [x] Kotlin-SDK
    - [ ] Php-SDK
    - [ ] Ruby-SDK

The Feature Flag service is experiencing cascading latency failures caused by database connection pool exhaustion. Slow queries from outlier teams for persons and cohorts are saturating the shared `persons_reader` and `non_persons_reader` pools, causing service-wide P99 connection acquisition times to spike from ~4ms to over 600ms.

**Core Issues:**
*   **Shared Infrastructure:** Person, cohort, and group queries share limited reader pools. Slow queries (5-10s) hold connections, starving fast queries (5ms) across all teams.
*   **Tight Coupling:** In `src/flags/flag_matching_utils.rs`, person and cohort queries execute sequentially on the same connection, preventing easy isolation.
*   **High Timeouts:** Current 5-10s statement timeouts and 20s acquisition timeouts allow pools to remain exhausted for long periods.

**Proposed Solution:**
*   **Phase 1 (Immediate):** Reduce statement timeouts (to 2s-3s) and connection acquisition timeouts (from 20s to 5s) to ensure fast failure and prevent queue depth growth.
*   **Phase 2 (Architectural):** Refactor the flag matching logic to release connections between operations and split `persons_reader` into two distinct pools: `persons_reader` (for person lookups) and `cohort_reader` (for cohort queries).
*   **Phase 3 (Optimization):** Address `non_persons_reader` exhaustion by investigating HyperCache misses and potentially tuning cache TTLs or prefetching.

**Key Metrics & Errors:**
*   **Error Types:** `query_canceled` due to statement timeouts; `flags_acquire_timeout_total` spikes.
*   **Latency:** Connection acquisition spikes to 400-670ms during exhaustion events.
*   **Pools Affected:** `persons_reader`, `non_persons_reader`, and `persons_writer`.

Improve Kubernetes probes for feature-flag pods to prevent unnecessary pod churn and cascading failures during high traffic. The existing `/_readiness` endpoint blocks on `pool.acquire()`, which can take up to 20 seconds under load, exceeding the Kubernetes 2-second probe timeout and causing healthy but busy pods to be removed from rotation.

The solution introduces a three-tier readiness check strategy: 
1. A "fast path" that returns OK immediately if any database connections exist (`pool.size() > 0`).
2. A cached reachability check (default 30s) to prevent reconnect storms.
3. A fallback probe with a 500ms timeout. 

Additionally, a new `/_startup` endpoint is implemented to perform a blocking parallel check of all database pools, ensuring connectivity before the pod accepts traffic. These changes decouple "busy" states from "broken" states, maintaining capacity during database pool exhaustion. Includes new configuration variables: `READINESS_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS` and `READINESS_VALIDATION_INTERVAL_MS`.

In the LLM analytics trace view, tool responses containing certain content—specifically Markdown files with embedded HTML or XML-like tags—are being replaced with an `Invalid XML content` error message. This issue occurs when the tool response is being rendered with "XML highlighting" enabled. The problem was observed when a tool returned a README file containing ``, `
`, and `
` tags. Disabling XML highlighting or switching to Markdown highlighting does not resolve the display error once triggered. The core issue lies in the trace viewer's XML parser failing to handle non-well-formed XML content (like standard HTML/Markdown) within tool outputs.

Add a warning pop up telling people that progress will be lost if they dont save the insight they're working on
### Feature request

## Is your feature request related to a problem?
Yes, I sometimes lose work because I'm working on an insight and I press the top left back arrow to go and do smth else (ex: create an action, cohort, etc) and I lose my work because I forget to save before leaving. 

## Describe the solution you'd like
A pop up warning people that they're about to lose any unsaved progress. I think that's the easiest way. 

## Describe alternatives you've considered
Automatic saving. But this seems like it might get confusing fast. As a user, I would prefer the first option.

Feature request to add a "this week" date filter option to PostHog insights. Currently, the date picker includes "today," "this month," and "last week," but lacks a "this week" option, forcing users to rely on the "last 7 days" rolling window. This inconsistency makes calendar-aligned weekly reporting difficult, as the rolling window does not match the start of the calendar week or align with the "last week" filter for week-over-week comparisons. The requested solution is a "this week" option that tracks data from the start of the current calendar week through the present, respecting the project's "week starts on" configuration. This affects the insights date picker and reporting consistency across the platform.

Show warning if incorrect templating usage detected (Hog vs. Liquid)
Currently users can get confused by `{` vs. `{{` and we don't do much to help them become aware of this.

Since workflows templating supports both Hog vs. Liquid (and the mode is set on the node level) we can easily show warnings if it looks like the wrong type is being used
